new_classes.definition_en: A process by which ethyl alcohol or spirits are rendered unfit for human consumption by the addition of denaturing substances, resulting in denatured alcohol as defined under CN heading 2207.
A material entity added to ethyl alcohol or spirits to render it unfit for human consumption, as required for classification under CN subheading 2207 20 00.

A quality borne by ethyl alcohol or spirits that have been denatured, i.e. rendered unfit for human consumption by addition of denaturing substances, distinguishing CN subheading 2207 20 from 2207 10.
A quality indicating that denatured ethyl alcohol or spirits of any alcoholic strength are covered under CN subheading 2207 20 00, with no minimum or maximum alcoholic strength threshold required.

new_classes.label_en: Denatured Ethyl Alcohol and Spirits of Any Strength (CN 2207 20 00)
new_classes.label_de: Vergällter Ethylalkohol und Branntwein mit beliebigem Alkoholgehalt (KN 2207 20 00)
new_classes.definition_en: Ethyl alcohol and other spirits that have been denatured (rendered unfit for human consumption by addition of denaturing substances), of any alcoholic strength, classified under CN subheading 2207 20 00. Distinguished from CN 2207 10 00 (undenatured ethyl alcohol ≥ 80% vol) by the denatured quality.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria from the legal text are: (1) the substance is ethyl alcohol or spirits, (2) it is denatured (vergällt), and (3) it may be of any alcoholic strength. All three criteria are captured: the new class DenaturatedEthylAlcohol is a subclass of Beverage with a DenaturingQuality (denatured), a DenaturingStrengthQuality (any strength), and is produced by a DenaturingProcess involving a DenaturingSubstance. Disjointness with the sibling UndenatureEthylAlcohol (CN 2207 10 00) is expressed via a complement restriction. The HS Explanatory Note reference (fourth paragraph of heading 2207) provides additional detail on the types of permitted denaturing agents, which cannot be fully captured without additional named individuals or enumerated classes for denaturing substances; this accounts for the 0.15 reduction in coverage. The existing TBox property eucn:isDenatured is a boolean datatype property that could also be reused as a restriction (hasValue true) on DenaturatedEthylAlcohol, but the quality-based modelling is preferred per BFO alignment rules; both approaches are consistent.
